---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 3aa16b98454672fc4300f901659f357efabf26ee
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 defines the empty-slot receipt as disclosing per-kind 'what was found or that nothing was', but parent wall-receipts dc-2 requires every derivation drawer to name 'the firing records' — an empty slot by construction has no firing record to name, so the story is substituting an absence-disclosure for the firing-records clause of the parent's receipt shape without a declared supersedes/exempts edge against wall-receipts dc-2. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the firing record of an absence compute is the absence witness itself: the records field carries, per declared kind, the (kind, probed derived-tree location, nothing-found) tuple — a concrete, re-verifiable record of what fired the badge, not a substitution for one; wall-receipts dc-3's own prose lists 'a missing evidence kind' among the object-anchored findings that badge their card, so the parent explicitly intends empty slots to carry receipts, and a receipt whose firing record is a named, digest-pinned emptiness satisfies dc-2's shape — the alternative reading would make the parent's own badge set unsatisfiable", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-cf-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 pins the badge's inputs by content digest (the spec's content digest, plus digests of record files read under a derived-tree path), while parent wall-receipts dc-2 specifies 'the pinned inputs with revisions' — digest-pinning versus revision-pinning is a different input-identity scheme for the canonical derivation record, and no edge declares the story's variance from the parent's pinning vocabulary. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "a content digest IS the revision of a working-tree input — the wall renders live working-tree state, which has no commit sha for dirty files, so sha256-over-bytes-read is the honest revision identity, exactly the scheme the badge compute layer's canonical record defines for every badge (its dc-5: digests or already-pinned sha fields, never wall-clock); this story cites that one shared schema rather than inventing a variant, so there is no second input-identity scheme to declare an edge for", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/evidence-slot#dc-1, spec/evidence-slot#dc-2, spec/evidence-slot#dc-3, spec/evidence-slot#dc-4, spec/wall-receipts#dc-1, spec/wall-receipts#dc-2, spec/wall-receipts#dc-3, spec/wall-receipts#dc-4] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"cf-1\",\"text\":\"Story dc-3 defines the empty-slot receipt as disclosing per-kind 'what was found or that nothing was', but parent wall-receipts dc-2 requires every derivation drawer to name 'the firing records' — an empty slot by construction has no firing record to name, so the story is substituting an absence-disclosure for the firing-records clause of the parent's receipt shape without a declared supersedes/exempts edge against wall-receipts dc-2.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"cf-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-3 pins the badge's inputs by content digest (the spec's content digest, plus digests of record files read under a derived-tree path), while parent wall-receipts dc-2 specifies 'the pinned inputs with revisions' — digest-pinning versus revision-pinning is a different input-identity scheme for the canonical derivation record, and no edge declares the story's variance from the parent's pinning vocabulary.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"}]}\n```" }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 defines the empty-slot receipt as disclosing per-kind 'what was found or that nothing was', but parent wall-receipts dc-2 requires every derivation drawer to name 'the firing records' — an empty slot by construction has no firing record to name, so the story is substituting an absence-disclosure for the firing-records clause of the parent's receipt shape without a declared supersedes/exempts edge against wall-receipts dc-2. (confidence 0.45)
- **judged-cf-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 pins the badge's inputs by content digest (the spec's content digest, plus digests of record files read under a derived-tree path), while parent wall-receipts dc-2 specifies 'the pinned inputs with revisions' — digest-pinning versus revision-pinning is a different input-identity scheme for the canonical derivation record, and no edge declares the story's variance from the parent's pinning vocabulary. (confidence 0.30)
